messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching]  R in intro stats
messages.body_plain:
Forwarded message: From: Bob Dobrow <rdobrow at carleton.edu>
> 
> We've taken our GUI-based SPSS lab manual and rewritten it for R. If 
> anyone would like to see it I can make it available. I'll probably need 
> to supplement it with some very basic intro tutorials or
> worksheets giving basic introduction.

I would be interested.  Could this be on the WWW?  You might be able
to use my handouts at

   http://www.statland.org/R/dirlist.htm

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] R in intro stats
messages.body_plain:
Although not used in intro stats, I have developed 2 tutorials for 
using R in psychology  for advanced undergraduates.
The first is a research methods course where they are expected to 
analyze some data.
The second is a personality research course where the examples are 
more geared to the types of problems they will find there.

http://personality-project.org/r/r.short.html
http://personality-project.org/r/r.205.tutorial.html

both of these are abstracted from a somewhat longer tutorial
http://personality-project.org/r/

Bill
